zh.lifeSummary: 當Emma Beauchamp於1108年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾時，她的父親Walter de Beauchamp38歲，母親Emmeline d'Abetot33歲。她於1124年與Orme Gulden在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1156年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Emma Beauchamp于1108年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾时，她的父亲Walter de Beauchamp38岁，母亲Emmeline d'Abetot33岁。她于1124年与Orme Gulden在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。她去世于1156年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma Beauchamp was born in 1108, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Walter de Beauchamp, was 38 and her mother, Emmeline d'Abetot, was 33. She married Orme Gulden in 1124, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died in 1156, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emma Beauchamp est née en 1108, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter de Beauchamp, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Emmeline d'Abetot, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Orme Gulden en 1124, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée en 1156, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Emma Beauchamp 1108 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter de Beauchamp 38 und ihre Mutter Emmeline d'Abetot 33. Sie hat 1124 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Orme Gulden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Sie starb 1156 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Emma Beauchamp è nata il nel 1108 nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Walter de Beauchamp, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Emmeline d'Abetot, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Orme Gulden nel 1124, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì nel 1156, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Emma Beauchampが1108年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Walter de Beauchampは38歳，母Emmeline d'Abetotは33歳であった。1124年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでOrme Guldenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼女は1156年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma Beauchamp(이)가 1108년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter de Beauchamp(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Emmeline d'Abetot(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1124년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Orme Gulden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1156년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Emma Beauchamp родилась в 1108 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Walter de Beauchamp было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Emmeline d'Abetot, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Orme Gulden в 1124 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла в 1156 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет.
